page: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8275CL processors running at 3.6 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 95,252.05 and a Passmark CPU score of 63,325.12. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 5,275.1 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 235.82 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in specialized tasks, including software compilation with a Geekbench clang score of 49,722.0, ray tracing at 58,945.0, and cryptography with a Passmark encryption score of 40,986.6. However, single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,456.0.

In terms of qualitative cost efficiency, the bare-metal configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the utility of the 96 dedicated vCPUs for high-density compute workloads. However, the lack of local storage requires external storage solutions, which may introduce latency. Additionally, while prompt processing for small LLM models is strong at 2,180.76 tokens/sec, text generation speeds are poor, making it less suitable for real-time generative AI without GPU acceleration. The server is best suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8275CL cores running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and efficient software compilation, though single-core metrics and LLM text generation speeds are average to poor. The bare-metal design eliminates hypervisor overhead, offering high resource density for compute-intensive tasks. This instance is suited for multi-threaded decompression, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and high memory bandwidth for intensive multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8275CL processors at 3.6 GHz
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
Bare-metal architecture eliminating hypervisor overhead for maximum resource density
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong performance in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
